topics.title: Spain and Argentina meet in the World Cup final
topics.summary: Spain and defending champion Argentina meet today in New Jersey to close the expanded 48-team World Cup. The matchup pairs European champion Spain with Lionel Messi's Argentina in the tournament's 104th and final game.
topics.discussionPrompt: A giant final can turn even casual viewers into instant experts. Are you watching for the soccer, the shared spectacle, or just the snacks and reactions?

topics.title: AWS glitch sends customers trillion-dollar bills
topics.summary: Amazon Web Services apologized after a global glitch showed some customers wildly incorrect bills, including totals as high as $1.5 trillion. The startling invoices affected accounts whose normal monthly costs could be less than a cup of coffee.
topics.discussionPrompt: Imagine opening a routine bill and seeing twelve extra zeroes. Would you panic, laugh, call support, or frame the screenshot first?

topics.title: Leaky pipes dwarf hosepipe-ban savings
topics.summary: Greenpeace UK says water companies in England and Wales lose 2.87 billion litres daily through leaking pipes—about five times what a nationwide hosepipe ban would save—as heat and low rainfall strain supplies.
topics.discussionPrompt: When a system leaks more water than households could save, where should responsibility start: personal habits, faster repairs, tougher rules, or all three?

topics.title: Ghana's movie posters become collectible art
topics.summary: Ghanaian artists are preserving a tradition of hand-painted movie posters whose wildly imaginative scenes often stray far beyond the actual films. Online collectors are helping revive demand for the colorful works, once used to advertise neighborhood video clubs.
topics.discussionPrompt: If you could commission an outrageously inaccurate poster for any movie, which one would you pick and what ridiculous scene should the artist add?
